IcpThresholds.description:
Workspace-configurable thresholds that define the ICP.

The ICP is advertisers who spend **consistently** but **do not iterate**
their creatives. Crucially this both requires the "stale long-runner"
signal *and* **excludes prolific testers** — advertisers already running
many distinct creatives / refreshing often (e.g. 20-100 UGC variations)
are disqualified, because they already test at scale and are not our buyer.

max_active_creatives.description: Hard ceiling on concurrently-active distinct creatives.
max_active_creatives.title: Max Active Creatives

max_creative_refresh_rate.description: Max new distinct creatives introduced per 30 days; above this the advertiser is iterating too actively to be a fit.
max_creative_refresh_rate.title: Max Creative Refresh Rate

max_distinct_creatives.description: Advertisers above this many distinct creatives are excluded (they already run lots of variations).
max_distinct_creatives.title: Max Distinct Creatives

IcpThresholds.title: IcpThresholds

post.description:
Login and get access token.

Both the access_token and refresh_token are set as httpOnly cookies so
they are never exposed to JavaScript (XSS-resistant). The access_token is
also returned in the JSON body for backward-compat callers (e.g. native
integrations); browser clients should ignore it and rely on the cookie.
Logout by revoking the refresh token and clearing the cookie.

schema.title: Response Logout Api V1 Auth Logout Post

get.description: Get current user info with default workspace.

post.description: Refresh access token using the refresh_token httpOnly cookie.
Register a new user.

Issue a short-lived ticket JWT for WebSocket authentication.

WebSocket connections cannot read httpOnly cookies in JS to forward as a
Bearer header, and cross-origin cookies on a WS handshake are unreliable.
The browser exchanges its httpOnly access cookie (verified by
``CurrentUser`` here) for a small short-lived JWT that it appends as a
query param on the WS URL. The ticket is single-purpose and expires in
one minute, limiting the blast radius if it ever leaks via referer or
server logs.

schema.title: Response Issue Ws Ticket Api V1 Auth Ws Ticket Post

get.description:
Liveness probe — process is up.

Intentionally does not touch external services. Use ``/readyz`` for that.
Resolve a short code, log the click, and 302 to the target URL.

Readiness probe — startup complete + Postgres + Redis reachable.

Returns HTTP 503 when:

* ``app.state.ready`` is ``False`` — the lifespan handler hasn't finished
  validating config and starting workers yet (or shutdown is in progress).
* Either Postgres or Redis fails / times out (2s budget each).
* Any expected worker is missing a fresh heartbeat key.

Orchestrators (Railway, Kubernetes) use this to hold traffic on the
previous container until the new one finishes booting and to drain a
container before it stops accepting requests.
Return the build's git SHA from ``RAILWAY_GIT_COMMIT_SHA``.
